  "description": "Carrot-Top Drugs Limited was incorporated in 1997. It started business as a retail pharmacy on October 30th, 2000. In 2005, the company started her own brands of innovative and quality fertility products with Predict Ovulation Prediction Kit being her first.

Today, Carrot-Top Drugs Limited is a household name for couples trying for a baby. The company is built on a tripod of hard work, transparency, and commitment to our numerous customers. Our passion for fertility and wellness is borne out of our desire to see couples fulfilled and happy. Our various communication channels are inundated with testimonies of obviously satisfied customers. Our strength is in our skilled, courteous, and very innovative manpower, as well as our distribution.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Medical Sales Representative

Job Location: Lagos Island, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Requirements


	Candidates must possess a Degree in Human Physiology, Anatomy, Pharmacology, Biochemistry, Microbiology or Biological Sciences.
	Have high moral / ethical standards.
	Willing to take instructions and execute diligently.
	Motivated by extra money to be made legitimately in the field.
	Must be proficient in Driving.
	Must be resident in Lagos Island.


Method of Application&nbsp;
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: admin@carrottopng.com using the Job Position as the subject of the email.",

Medical Sales Representative Job at Carrot-Top Drugs Limited




        
    

        
        Carrot-Top Drugs Limited was incorporated in 1997. It started business as a retail pharmacy on October 30th, 2000. In 2005, the company started her own brands of innovative and quality fertility products with Predict Ovulation Prediction Kit being her first.

Today, Carrot-Top Drugs Limited is a household name for couples trying for a baby. The company is built on a tripod of hard work, transparency, and commitment to our numerous customers. Our passion for fertility and wellness is borne out of our desire to see couples fulfilled and happy. Our various communication channels are inundated with testimonies of obviously satisfied customers. Our strength is in our skilled, courteous, and very innovative manpower, as well as our distribution.
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    



We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Medical Sales Representative

Job Location: Lagos Island, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Requirements


	Candidates must possess a Degree in Human Physiology, Anatomy, Pharmacology, Biochemistry, Microbiology or Biological Sciences.
	Have high moral / ethical standards.
	Willing to take instructions and execute diligently.
	Motivated by extra money to be made legitimately in the field.
	Must be proficient in Driving.
	Must be resident in Lagos Island.


Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the email.    

    

        Click here to Apply

{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Head, Internal Audit & Risk",
  "description": "Amaiden Energy Nigeria Limited (formerly Moody International Nigeria Limited) was established in 1996 as a partnership between Moody International Group and Nigerian investors. Since commencing operations in March 1997 the company continues to grow and exerts its presence in numerous Projects in the Nigeria Oil and Gas Industry.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Head, Internal Audit &amp; Risk

Job Location: Lagos

Description


	The Head, Internal Audit &amp; Risk is a senior, Board-facing role providing independent assurance to the Board Audit Committee.
	The job holder will be responsible for identifying, assessing, and mitigating risks that could adversely affect the company&#39;s operations, financial performance, reputation, and legal standing.
	The job holder will be responsible for ensuring that the risks faced by the organization are mitigated and all activities are conducted in line with the Board&#39;s risk appetite and in full compliance with regulatory requirements.
	The job holder will lead and manage internal audit activities, ensuring that the organization operates in accordance with internal policies and regulatory requirements.
	This role involves developing audit plans, supervising audit teams, conducting risk assessments, and providing strategic insights to improve internal controls and governance.


Corporate Risk Management:


	Conduct thorough risk assessments, identifying potential threats and vulnerabilities to the organization&rsquo;s operations, assets, and reputation.
	Analyse internal and external data to recognize emerging risks.
	Develop risk maps and heat charts to prioritize risks based on
	potential impact and likelihood.
	Design and implement risk management strategies that minimize
	exposure to risk, including loss prevention, contingency planning,
	and risk transfer options.
	Recommend and ensure risk mitigation processes are integrated
	into business decisions, processes, and contracts.
	Collaborate with various departments to implement risk controls.
	Monitor risk management practices and regularly update risk mitigation measures as necessary.
	Prepare risk reports for senior management and stakeholders,highlighting potential risk areas and recommending corrective actions.
	Ensure compliance with risk management policies, standards, and regulations.
	Develop and maintain business continuity and disaster recovery plans.
	Conduct simulations and crisis management exercises to ensure readiness in case of emergencies.
	Stay current with risk-related regulations and compliance requirements applicable to the organization&rsquo;s (and other entities)industries.
	Ensure that the organization is compliant with all legal, regulatory,and contractual obligations.
	Lead risk awareness programs and training sessions for employees at all levels.
	Promote a risk-conscious culture throughout the organization fromthe group company to all entities.
	Work closely with senior leadership, auditors, legal teams, and external stakeholders to align risk management with the organization&rsquo;s objectives.
	Act as the point of contact for external regulators, auditors, and other external/ third-party risk management entities.


Internal Audit &amp; Control:


	Develop, lead, and execute the Company&rsquo;s internal audit function,
	providing independent and objective assurance to the Board Audit
	Committee and Management on the effectiveness of governance,
	risk management, and internal control processes across the Group.
	Develop and implement a risk-based annual Internal Audit Plan,
	aligned with the Company&rsquo;s strategic objectives, risk profile, and
	regulatory requirements, for approval by the Board Audit Committee.
	Lead and execute internal audit reviews across financial,
	operational, compliance, IT, and project-related areas, including
	subsidiaries, joint ventures, and special purpose vehicles, as
	applicable.
	Assess the adequacy and effectiveness of internal controls, financial
	reporting processes, and operational controls, and recommend
	practical improvements where gaps are identified.
	Prepare clear, concise, and actionable internal audit reports,
	highlighting key findings, root causes, risk implications, and
	management action plans
	&nbsp;Monitor and follow up on audit findings and agreed remediation
	actions, tracking implementation and reporting progress to
	Management and the Board Audit Committee.
	Coordinate effectively with external auditors, regulators, and other
	assurance providers, ensuring appropriate reliance, avoidance of
	duplication, and timely resolution of issues.


Other Responsibilities:


	Provide all services related to the job role to client&#39;s subsidiary
	companies.
	Perform all other duties that are necessary and reasonably attached
	to the Head, Internal Audit &amp; Risk position and the fulfillment of its
	responsibilities.
	Any other responsibilities as assigned by the Chairman, Board Audit
	Committee.


Job Requirements
Qualifications:


	Bachelor&#39;s Degree in Accounting, Risk Management, Economics,Finance,&nbsp;&nbsp; Business Administration.
	Masters&rsquo; degree in Finance, Risk Management, Economics, or an
	MBA will be an advantage.
	Professional certifications such as Institute of Risk Management (IRM), PMI-PMI, RIMS - Certified Risk Management Professional (CMP), ACAMS: Certified Anti-Money Laundering Specialist, Certified Risk Manager (CRM), Certified Internal Auditor (CIA), Certified Public Accountant (CPA), or similar.


Experience:


	At least 12-15 years of experience in a team lead or leadership position in risk&nbsp;&nbsp; management, compliance, Internal Audit or a related field in an oil &amp; gas exploration &amp; production organization.
	At least 5 years in a senior management position.
	Previous experience in Internal Control.
	Previous experience in an oil and gas organization is mandatory
	Previous experience in any of the big four international audit firms will be an advantage.


Knowledge, Skills &amp; Competencies:


	Strong expertise in internal audit, enterprise risk management, and
	internal control frameworks, with practical application in oil and gas
	and asset-intensive environments.
	&nbsp;Sound knowledge of IFRS, financial controls, treasury operations,
	and project-based financial structures, including Joint Ventures and
	Production Sharing Contract (PSC) environments.
	&nbsp;Proven ability to identify, assess, and articulate risks clearly, and
	translate audit findings into practical, value-adding
	recommendations.
",
  "datePosted": "2026-05-27",
  "validThrough": "2026-06-13",
  "employmentType": "Full-Time",
  "hiringOrganization": {
    "@type": "Organization",
    "name": "Amaiden Energy Nigeria Limited"
    
  },

Head, Internal Audit & Risk Job at Amaiden Energy Nigeria Limited




        
    

        
        Amaiden Energy Nigeria Limited (formerly Moody International Nigeria Limited) was established in 1996 as a partnership between Moody International Group and Nigerian investors. Since commencing operations in March 1997 the company continues to grow and exerts its presence in numerous Projects in the Nigeria Oil and Gas Industry.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Head, Internal Audit & Risk

Job Location: Lagos

Description
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	The Head, Internal Audit & Risk is a senior, Board-facing role providing independent assurance to the Board Audit Committee.
	The job holder will be responsible for identifying, assessing, and mitigating risks that could adversely affect the company's operations, financial performance, reputation, and legal standing.
	The job holder will be responsible for ensuring that the risks faced by the organization are mitigated and all activities are conducted in line with the Board's risk appetite and in full compliance with regulatory requirements.
	The job holder will lead and manage internal audit activities, ensuring that the organization operates in accordance with internal policies and regulatory requirements.
	This role involves developing audit plans, supervising audit teams, conducting risk assessments, and providing strategic insights to improve internal controls and governance.


Corporate Risk Management:


	Conduct thorough risk assessments, identifying potential threats and vulnerabilities to the organization’s operations, assets, and reputation.
	Analyse internal and external data to recognize emerging risks.
	Develop risk maps and heat charts to prioritize risks based on
	potential impact and likelihood.
	Design and implement risk management strategies that minimize
	exposure to risk, including loss prevention, contingency planning,
	and risk transfer options.
	Recommend and ensure risk mitigation processes are integrated
	into business decisions, processes, and contracts.
	Collaborate with various departments to implement risk controls.
	Monitor risk management practices and regularly update risk mitigation measures as necessary.
	Prepare risk reports for senior management and stakeholders,highlighting potential risk areas and recommending corrective actions.
	Ensure compliance with risk management policies, standards, and regulations.
	Develop and maintain business continuity and disaster recovery plans.
	Conduct simulations and crisis management exercises to ensure readiness in case of emergencies.
	Stay current with risk-related regulations and compliance requirements applicable to the organization’s (and other entities)industries.
	Ensure that the organization is compliant with all legal, regulatory,and contractual obligations.
	Lead risk awareness programs and training sessions for employees at all levels.
	Promote a risk-conscious culture throughout the organization fromthe group company to all entities.
	Work closely with senior leadership, auditors, legal teams, and external stakeholders to align risk management with the organization’s objectives.
	Act as the point of contact for external regulators, auditors, and other external/ third-party risk management entities.


Internal Audit & Control:


	Develop, lead, and execute the Company’s internal audit function,
	providing independent and objective assurance to the Board Audit
	Committee and Management on the effectiveness of governance,
	risk management, and internal control processes across the Group.
	Develop and implement a risk-based annual Internal Audit Plan,
	aligned with the Company’s strategic objectives, risk profile, and
	regulatory requirements, for approval by the Board Audit Committee.
	Lead and execute internal audit reviews across financial,
	operational, compliance, IT, and project-related areas, including
	subsidiaries, joint ventures, and special purpose vehicles, as
	applicable.
	Assess the adequacy and effectiveness of internal controls, financial
	reporting processes, and operational controls, and recommend
	practical improvements where gaps are identified.
	Prepare clear, concise, and actionable internal audit reports,
	highlighting key findings, root causes, risk implications, and
	management action plans
	 Monitor and follow up on audit findings and agreed remediation
	actions, tracking implementation and reporting progress to
	Management and the Board Audit Committee.
	Coordinate effectively with external auditors, regulators, and other
	assurance providers, ensuring appropriate reliance, avoidance of
	duplication, and timely resolution of issues.


Other Responsibilities:


	Provide all services related to the job role to client's subsidiary
	companies.
	Perform all other duties that are necessary and reasonably attached
	to the Head, Internal Audit & Risk position and the fulfillment of its
	responsibilities.
	Any other responsibilities as assigned by the Chairman, Board Audit
	Committee.


Job Requirements
Qualifications:


	Bachelor's Degree in Accounting, Risk Management, Economics,Finance,   Business Administration.
	Masters’ degree in Finance, Risk Management, Economics, or an
	MBA will be an advantage.
	Professional certifications such as Institute of Risk Management (IRM), PMI-PMI, RIMS - Certified Risk Management Professional (CMP), ACAMS: Certified Anti-Money Laundering Specialist, Certified Risk Manager (CRM), Certified Internal Auditor (CIA), Certified Public Accountant (CPA), or similar.


Experience:


	At least 12-15 years of experience in a team lead or leadership position in risk   management, compliance, Internal Audit or a related field in an oil & gas exploration & production organization.
	At least 5 years in a senior management position.
	Previous experience in Internal Control.
	Previous experience in an oil and gas organization is mandatory
	Previous experience in any of the big four international audit firms will be an advantage.


Knowledge, Skills & Competencies:


	Strong expertise in internal audit, enterprise risk management, and
	internal control frameworks, with practical application in oil and gas
	and asset-intensive environments.
	 Sound knowledge of IFRS, financial controls, treasury operations,
	and project-based financial structures, including Joint Ventures and
	Production Sharing Contract (PSC) environments.
	 Proven ability to identify, assess, and articulate risks clearly, and
	translate audit findings into practical, value-adding
	recommendations.

title: Job Vacancies at Career Breedge Consultancy Limited
content:
Career Breedge Consultancy Limited is a comprehensive Talent Development and Management organization committed to empowering young, aspiring professionals and equipping businesses with bespoke HR solutions.

Through world-class professional training, counseling, and skills development programs, we help job seekers and career professionals become sought-after talents while assisting businesses in optimizing their workforce for maximum productivity.

We are recruiting to fill the following positions below:

1.) Operations / Marketing Officer

Job Location: Port Harcourt, Rivers
Employment Type: Full-time

About the Role
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	We are seeking a smart, proactive, and result-driven Operations/Marketing Officer who will play a key role in daily operations and directly source new catering contracts to grow the business.


Key Responsibilities


	Oversee and coordinate daily operations for catering and camp services
	Drive business development efforts by sourcing and securing new catering jobs
	Build and maintain strong client relationships across all service areas
	Prepare quotations, proposals, and detailed business reports
	Develop and execute effective marketing and growth strategies
	Monitor operational expenses and support cost-control initiatives
	Ensure strict compliance with HSE and food safety standards
	Prepare daily, weekly, and monthly operational reports for management.


Requirements


	HND / B.Sc Degree in Marketing, Business Administration, Hospitality Management, or a related field
	3 – 6 years of proven experience in catering operations or similar roles
	Strong communication, negotiation, and organizational skills
	Knowledge of cost control, marketing strategies, and reporting
	Proficiency in Microsoft Office (Word, Excel, PowerPoint)
	Experience in industrial catering services is a strong advantage
	Age: 35–40 years.


Salary
N250,000 - N300,000 monthly.

Application Closing Date: 30th June, 2026.

Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: [email protected] and [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the mail.

 

 

2.) Brand Manager

Job Location: Port Harcourt, Rivers
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Summary


	We are seeking a creative, strategic, and result-driven Brand Manager to lead and strengthen the company’s brand presence and corporate communications.


Duities and Responsibilities


	Develop and execute brand growth strategies
	Create engaging digital and offline content for marketing campaigns
	Handle video editing and multimedia brand communications
	Present and represent the brand on radio, TV, and corporate platforms
	Manage the company’s social media and digital marketing activities
	Write blogs, scripts, marketing copies, and promotional materials
	Ensure brand consistency and compliance across all channels
	Lead marketing campaigns and monitor performance outcomes
	Conduct presentations for corporate clients, partners, and investors
	Build and maintain strategic business relationships
	Train and supervise brand and marketing team members
	Coordinate public relations and corporate image management activities
	Drive customer engagement and brand visibility initiatives


Qualifications


	Graduate degree in a relevant field
	Minimum of 3 years of experience in Corporate Brand Management, Business Management, or Media Brand Management.


Requirements:


	Strong content creation skills
	Video editing expertise
	Radio & TV presentation skills
	Blog writing ability
	Digital marketing knowledge
	Leadership and team management skills
	Training and coaching skills
	Brand compliance management
	B2B presentation skills
	Corporate marketing experience
	Relationship management skills.


Application Closing Date: 31st May, 2026. 

Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: [email protected] and copy [email protected]  using the Job Position as the subject of the mail.

 

 

3.) Marketing Executive

Job Location: Port Harcourt, Rivers
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Description


	We are seeking a result-driven and experienced Marketing Executives with a strong passion for real estate and a proven ability to lead, mobilize, and transform teams into high-performing sales professionals.


Key Responsibilities


	Develop and execute strategic marketing plans to drive property sales and brand visibility
	Recruit, train, and manage a team of marketers and sales executives
	Set clear targets and performance benchmarks for the team
	Drive daily, weekly, and monthly sales performance
	Coach and mentor team members into top-performing marketers
	Build and maintain strategic partnerships and client relationships
	Oversee lead generation, conversion, and closing processes
	Monitor market trends and provide insights for competitive advantage
	Ensure strict adherence to company brand and marketing standards
	Prepare and present performance reports to management.


Experience & Qualifications


	Bachelor’s Degree in Marketing, Business Administration, or related field.
	Minimum of 2 years of marketing experience
	Prior experience in: Banking sector, Insurance marketing
	Real estate (added advantage)
	Strong background in sales strategy, team leadership, and revenue generation
	Excellent communication, negotiation, and interpersonal skills.


Key Requirements:


	Must be based in or willing to work in Port Harcourt
	Ready to resume immediately
	Strong passion for marketing and real estate
	Proven ability to lead, coach, and mentor teams
	Deep understanding of targets, KPIs, and commission-based structures.


What We Offer


	Competitive basic salary + attractive commission structure (Negotiable)
	Opportunity to lead a dynamic and ambitious team
	Career growth within a fast-expanding real estate company
	Monthly performance-based incentives and recognition.


Application Closing Date: 30th June, 2026.

Method of Application
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: [email protected] and copy: [email protected] using "Application for Marketing Executive" as the subject of the mail.

     

    

        Click here to Apply

{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Aesthetic Doctor",
  "description": "Montaigne Place is a leading luxury retail company that offers an exquisite range of cosmetics, fragrances, skincare, lifestyle products, spa and wellness services. Established in 1995, Montaigne Place is a trading name of Montaigne AH. Ltd. began its journey in the Nigerian beauty sector as exclusive agents and distributors of the prestigious Clarins brand.

Today, Montaigne Place operations extend across retail, wholesale, spa &amp; wellness, with its flagship retail division, Montaigne Place, boasting a network of Forty (40) outlets throughout Nigeria. These outlets are strategically located in Lagos, the country&#39;s commercial hub, as well as in Abuja, the capital city, and Port Harcourt, along with other prominent cities. Montaigne Place is renowned for curating an impressive collection of sought-after brands in fragrances, skincare, and cosmetics. Its exclusive portfolio comprises more than a dozen top-tier names, including Creed, Roja Parfum, Ajmal, The Spirit of Dubai, Parfums de Marly, Atelier des Ors, Yves Rocher, Murad and more. In addition to these exclusive brands, the company also stocks mainstream favorites such as Clarins, Chanel, YSL, Armani, Bulgari, Lancome and others.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Aesthetic Doctor
Job Location: Victoria Island, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Responsibilities


	This role is responsible for delivering high-quality, non-surgical cosmetic and aesthetic treatments to clients, ensuring the highest standards of care, safety, and client satisfaction.
	The role includes conducting consultations, diagnosing skin and aesthetic concerns, administering procedures such as Botox, dermal fillers, chemical peels, and other advanced treatments, and providing aftercare guidance.
	The role maintains clinical compliance, supports continuous improvement of service offerings, and upholds the company&rsquo;s reputation as a trusted leader in the beauty and wellness space.
	Additionally, the role ensures that all procedures are performed safely and ethically in line with regulatory requirements and clinic protocols, while fostering client trust, enhancing patient education, and driving repeat patronage through exceptional clinical outcomes and customer experience.
	Ensure the safe, ethical, and effective delivery of cosmetic treatments and procedures in line with regulatory standards and clinic protocols.
	Drive client trust, repeat patronage, and clinic reputation through excellent clinical outcomes, patient education, and exceptional customer experience.
	Conduct thorough consultations, skin assessments, and medical evaluations to determine appropriate aesthetic treatment plans.
	Perform advanced aesthetic procedures such as injectables (Botox, fillers), chemical peels, micro-needling, PRP, laser treatments, body contouring, and other non-surgical cosmetic procedures approved by the clinic.
	Develop personalized treatment plans tailored to clients&rsquo; goals, health history, and skin conditions.
	Ensure the highest standards of clinical safety, infection control, hygiene, and regulatory compliance across all procedures.
	Provide post-treatment care instructions and follow up with clients to monitor progress and manage expected outcomes.
	Maintain detailed and accurate patient documentation in compliance with medical regulations and clinic policies.
	Collaborate closely with clinic management, customer service, sales, and marketing to support client education, content creation, and brand positioning.
	Develop treatment protocols, SOPs, and service quality standards.
	Continuously research emerging technologies, techniques, and global best practices in aesthetic medicine.
	Support the training, mentoring, and skill development of aesthetic nurses and clinical support staff.
	Handle escalation of complex medical/aesthetic cases and provide professional medical judgment when complications arise.
	Represent the clinic professionally during medical events, webinars, and internal/external knowledge-sharing sessions.


Requirement


	Candidates should possess a Bachelor&#39;s Degree.


Salary
N1,500,000 - N2,200,000 per month.&nbsp;

Method of Application&nbsp;
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: hrexecutive4@montaigneplace.com using the Job Position as the subject of the email.",
  "datePosted": "2026-05-27",
  "validThrough": "2026-06-21",

    "name": "Montaigne Place"
    
  },

Aesthetic Doctor Job at Montaigne Place




        
    

        
        Montaigne Place is a leading luxury retail company that offers an exquisite range of cosmetics, fragrances, skincare, lifestyle products, spa and wellness services. Established in 1995, Montaigne Place is a trading name of Montaigne AH. Ltd. began its journey in the Nigerian beauty sector as exclusive agents and distributors of the prestigious Clarins brand.
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    



Today, Montaigne Place operations extend across retail, wholesale, spa & wellness, with its flagship retail division, Montaigne Place, boasting a network of Forty (40) outlets throughout Nigeria. These outlets are strategically located in Lagos, the country's commercial hub, as well as in Abuja, the capital city, and Port Harcourt, along with other prominent cities. Montaigne Place is renowned for curating an impressive collection of sought-after brands in fragrances, skincare, and cosmetics. Its exclusive portfolio comprises more than a dozen top-tier names, including Creed, Roja Parfum, Ajmal, The Spirit of Dubai, Parfums de Marly, Atelier des Ors, Yves Rocher, Murad and more. In addition to these exclusive brands, the company also stocks mainstream favorites such as Clarins, Chanel, YSL, Armani, Bulgari, Lancome and others.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Aesthetic Doctor
Job Location: Victoria Island, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Responsibilities


	This role is responsible for delivering high-quality, non-surgical cosmetic and aesthetic treatments to clients, ensuring the highest standards of care, safety, and client satisfaction.
	The role includes conducting consultations, diagnosing skin and aesthetic concerns, administering procedures such as Botox, dermal fillers, chemical peels, and other advanced treatments, and providing aftercare guidance.
	The role maintains clinical compliance, supports continuous improvement of service offerings, and upholds the company’s reputation as a trusted leader in the beauty and wellness space.
	Additionally, the role ensures that all procedures are performed safely and ethically in line with regulatory requirements and clinic protocols, while fostering client trust, enhancing patient education, and driving repeat patronage through exceptional clinical outcomes and customer experience.
	Ensure the safe, ethical, and effective delivery of cosmetic treatments and procedures in line with regulatory standards and clinic protocols.
	Drive client trust, repeat patronage, and clinic reputation through excellent clinical outcomes, patient education, and exceptional customer experience.
	Conduct thorough consultations, skin assessments, and medical evaluations to determine appropriate aesthetic treatment plans.
	Perform advanced aesthetic procedures such as injectables (Botox, fillers), chemical peels, micro-needling, PRP, laser treatments, body contouring, and other non-surgical cosmetic procedures approved by the clinic.
	Develop personalized treatment plans tailored to clients’ goals, health history, and skin conditions.
	Ensure the highest standards of clinical safety, infection control, hygiene, and regulatory compliance across all procedures.
	Provide post-treatment care instructions and follow up with clients to monitor progress and manage expected outcomes.
	Maintain detailed and accurate patient documentation in compliance with medical regulations and clinic policies.
	Collaborate closely with clinic management, customer service, sales, and marketing to support client education, content creation, and brand positioning.
	Develop treatment protocols, SOPs, and service quality standards.
	Continuously research emerging technologies, techniques, and global best practices in aesthetic medicine.
	Support the training, mentoring, and skill development of aesthetic nurses and clinical support staff.
	Handle escalation of complex medical/aesthetic cases and provide professional medical judgment when complications arise.
	Represent the clinic professionally during medical events, webinars, and internal/external knowledge-sharing sessions.


Requirement


	Candidates should possess a Bachelor's Degree.


Salary
N1,500,000 - N2,200,000 per month. 

Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the email.    

    

        Click here to Apply
